tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'linear_issues'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests'"}

text: Good. Now I'll find Linear issues that are 'done' AND are referenced by at least one PR's closes_linear, but none of those PRs are 'merged'.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT li.id
FROM linear_issues li
WHERE li.state = 'done'
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM github_pull_requests pr
    WHERE pr.closes_linear = li.id
  )
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM github_pull_requests pr
    WHERE pr.closes_linear = li.id
      AND pr.state = 'merged'
  )
ORDER BY li.id"}

text: Let me verify the data by checking a sample of issues and PRs to make sure the data is as expected.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, state FROM linear_issues WHERE state = 'done' LIMIT 10"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT pr.number, pr.state, pr.closes_linear FROM github_pull_requests pr WHERE pr.closes_linear IS NOT NULL LIMIT 20"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT li.id, li.state, 
  (SELECT string_agg(pr.number::text || ':' || pr.state, ', ') 
   FROM github_pull_requests pr 
   WHERE pr.closes_linear = li.id) AS referencing_prs
FROM linear_issues li
WHERE li.state = 'done'
ORDER BY li.id"}

text: Interesting — my first query returned empty, but I can see ENG-142 is 'done' with only open PRs claiming to close it. Let me re-run the precise query to verify:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT li.id
FROM linear_issues li
WHERE li.state = 'done'
  AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM github_pull_requests pr WHERE pr.closes_linear = li.id)
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM github_pull_requests pr WHERE pr.closes_linear = li.id AND pr.state = 'merged')
ORDER BY li.id"}

text: Ah wow! ENG-142 has MANY PRs claiming to close it — including several that are 'merged' (numbers 1005, 1006, 1014, 1016, 1023, 1029). So my first query was correct — ENG-142 IS closed by a merged PR, so it's excluded. The earlier quick samp…(+239)

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT li.id, 
  COUNT(pr_all.number) AS total_ref_prs,
  COUNT(pr_merged.number) AS merged_prs
FROM linear_issues li
LEFT JOIN github_pull_requests pr_all ON pr_all.closes_linear = li.id
LEFT JOIN github_pull_requests pr_merged ON pr_merged.closes_linear = li.id AND pr_merged.state = 'merged'
WHERE li.state = 'done'
GROUP BY li.id
HAVING COUNT(pr_all.number) > 0
ORDER BY li.id"}
